text:
```json
{
  "type": "DRAG_FILL",
  "title": "Challenge: Apply IAM Demystified",
  "lessonText": "Apply your knowledge of IAM users and groups to configure access permissions.",
  "data": {
    "challenges": [
      {
        "instruction": "Complete the following statements about IAM users and groups by dragging the correct terms into the blanks. Use the definitions from the 'IAM Demystified Essentials' learning card to help you.",
        "codeTemplate": "An IAM _______ represents an individual person or application that interacts with AWS.\nIAM _______ are collections of users that can be assigned identical permissions.\nEach IAM user has unique security _______.",
        "options": [
          "Group",
          "credentials",
          "User",
          "Policies"
        ],
        "correctAnswer": "User,Group,credentials",
        "explanation": "IAM Users represent individual entities. IAM Groups are collections of users for simplified permission management. Each IAM user requires unique security credentials for authentication."
      },
      {
        "instruction": "Fill in the blanks to describe how IAM groups simplify permission management. Refer to 'IAM Demystified: A Practical Walkthrough' if needed.",
        "codeTemplate": "IAM groups simplify permission management by allowing you to assign _______ to a group instead of individual _______. This ensures consistent _______ across all users within the group.",
        "options": [
          "policies",
          "users",
          "permissions",
          "access"
        ],
        "correctAnswer": "permissions,users,access",
        "explanation": "IAM groups streamline permission management by enabling the assignment of permissions to a group, rather than individual users, ensuring consistent access across the group."
      },
      {
        "instruction": "Complete the sentence about IAM users. Use the 'IAM Demystified: IAM Users: Detailed Explanation' concept breakdown to help you.",
        "codeTemplate": "IAM _______ can be assigned _______ to control their access to AWS resources.",
        "options": [
          "roles",
          "resources",
          "users",
          "policies"
        ],
        "correctAnswer": "users,policies",
        "explanation": "IAM users can be assigned policies to define and control their access to AWS resources, ensuring secure and controlled interactions."
      }
    ]
  }
}
```
